meaning: 'full-length robe'; from Gk. podērē, acc. sg. of podērēs 'robe that falls to the feet', used in the Septuagint for 'the high priest's robe'.
origin_explanation: from Gk. podērē, acc. sg. of podērēs 'robe that falls to the feet', used in the Septuagint for 'the high priest's robe'.
from Gk. epagomenai 'intercalated days'; also Chr.-Ar. abīgūmānis, Coptic epagomene.

meaning: name of the ninth Coptic month (corresponds to G. ganbot); a corruption of Coptic pakhōn, pašons.
origin_explanation: a corruption of Coptic pakhōn, pašons.
from Gk. paksamās 'biscuit'; also in Tna. baq'asmat, Te. baqsāwit, Har. buqsumāt, Ar. buqsumāt. See also paskimā.
from Gk. pykson, gen. pl. of pyksos 'box tree'; see also tekson. For the alternance p:t, see pngl:tngl.
either from Gk. plintheion, pl. plintheia 'brickworks', or from Gk. plinthion 'brick mold'. Tayyā's translation 'brick' is not precise.
ethiopic_word: ጰልንትያ፣ ጵልንቲያ፣ ጵልንትዩ፣ ጵላንቲያ፣ ጰለንቲያ፣ ጰላንቲያ፣ ጰላንትያ፣ ጵልትንያ

origin_explanation: see also palātanos.
perhaps confused with pīltānos.
from Gk. pelekan 'pelican' (Hommel 1887: xxx, 49).

meaning: (T) palis 'capital city, (T) bishop'; The meaning 'bishop' is either due to a scribal error or it is an abbreviation of mētropolitēs 'the bishop of the capital'.
origin_explanation: from Gk. polis 'town, city'. The meaning 'bishop' is either due to a scribal error or it is an abbreviation of mētropolitēs 'the bishop of the capital'.
from Gk. platanos 'plane tree'. See also palānatos, pīlṭānos, balṭonos.

meaning: (T) 'perfume'; could it be confused with palāṭānos?
origin_explanation: could it be confused with palāṭānos?
from Gk. peltās, acc. pl. of peltē 'shield'.

meaning: (Gr.351) 'iron'; perhaps instead of peryon I, below.
origin_explanation: perhaps instead of peryon I, below.

meaning: (T.M) 'comment, ex-pound, translate, recite, interpret, repeat from memory';
origin_explanation: pangālē (K*) 'commentary, report, translation, interpretation';
from Gk. apangellein 'announce, inform, expound, interpret'. Note the creation of the Geez forms pangālē, mapangal. See also tngl.
transcription of Heb. panīnā 'pearl'.
from Gk. pentēkostē (sc. hēmera) 'the fiftieth day', Pentecost'; also Chr.-Ar. bantīqistī.
seems to be onomatopoetic.

meaning: (T) pappirā 'purple, purple powder, red dye used for dyeing cloth or fly whisks';
origin_explanation: see also papirā.

meaning: (pl. pāppāsāt, pāppāsāwəst) 'metropolitan, patriarch (of the church), bishop'; liqa pāppās, liqa pāppāsāt 'archbishop'; 'apappāsa, 'apāppāsa 'appoint an archbishop'; pappus 'an appointed metropolitan'; pappāsē 'episcopate, patriarchate'; pappāsannā 'episcopacy, office of the patriarch'; liqa pappāsannā 'archbishopric';
origin_explanation: from Gk. papas 'father, title of priests'; also Chr.-Ar. bābā.
from Gk. perdiks 'partridge'.
corrupt from porfaryonā.
from Gk. porphyrion 'water hen'. See also parfānā.
see also perkā.
from Gk. paralia (sc. gē 'land') 'land by the sea'.
from Gk. parinos 'from Paros', the Greek island famous for its marble. See also paranso.

meaning: (K) 'precious stone'; variant or misreading of pārenu.
origin_explanation: variant or misreading of pārenu.
from Gk. pyrobolou, gen. sg. of pyrobolos 'flint stone'.
from Gk. probata, pl. of probaton 'sheep, goats'. See also pirupāṭqe, below.
from Gk. probatikē, fem. of probatikos 'of the sheep'. See also perbāṭeqe, pərupāṭā.

meaning: the Holy Spirit as intercessor, Pentecost, Transfiguration (celebrated the 13th of Nahāsē);
origin_explanation: from Gk. paraklētos 'intercessor, the Holy Spirit as intercessor'; also Chr.-Ar. bārāqlīṭ.

meaning: (T.M) 'write'; perhaps from Gk. programma 'written order, edict' (G. Salinger). In this case Geez may have created a denominative.
origin_explanation: perhaps from Gk. programma 'written order, edict' (G. Salinger). In this case Geez may have created a denominative.
from Gk. prosphorā 'offering, Eucharist'; also in Chr.-Ar. 'abrusfār.
perhaps from Gk. periokhē 'circumscribed place' (Salinger).
from Gk. prasios (sc. lithos) 'light-green stone, emerald'
from Gk. prionos, prioni, gen. and dat. sg. of priōn 'saw' (see also Hommel 1877:xxix, 84). As for 'shackles', it is an interpretation of the original meaning 'saw'.

meaning: (T) paryon 'kind of oak, (T.Sw. in D 1252) pomegranate'
origin_explanation: from Gk. prinos, acc. prinon 'holm oak'. The meaning 'pomegranate' seems to be a misunderstanding.
corrupt from Gk. presbyteros 'elder, priest'. See also porezbiṭar, ṭarbezēr.
from Gk. paskha, transcription of Aram. pashā. See also pāsikā.
from Gk. pastophoria, nom. acc. pl. of pastophorion 'priest's quarters, sacristy'.
Grimme 1914:261 identifies it with Heb. pašaṭ 'strip off'. Aram. Syr. pašaṭ 'spread out'.

meaning: (K.DTW 1007) 'pure perfume'; pastages nardos (Gr.351) 'genuine spikenard'; it refers to the Holy Spirit;
origin_explanation: from Gk. pistikēs, gen. sg. fem. of pistikos 'genuine, of pure quality'; also in Amh. fəsṭəq 'kind of perfume'.
from Gk. pitys 'pine tree', acc. pl. pityas.
from Gk. batos 'bramble bush'. See also bāṭos.
from Gk. peukinos 'made from pine wood', peukina '(things) made from pine wood'.
ethiopic_word: ጰውቂና፣ ጰውቄና፣ ጳውቄና፣ ጰውቄኔ፣ ጴውቄና፣ ጴውቂኖስ፣ ጰውቂኖ

meaning: name of the tenth Coptic month (corresponds to G. sane)
origin_explanation: from Coptic paōne, paane. See also pāhen, barunāh.
from Gk. pastophoros, lit. 'carrier of a shrine' (L. Guerrier-S. Grébaut, Le testament en Galilée de notre-Seigneur Jesus-Christ, p. 134).
from Gk. topazion 'chrysolite, topaz', with the loss of to wrongly mistaken for the Greek article.
